name: Chinese Tuna Steaks on the Grill
recipeInstructions:
Contributed to the echo by: Bill Birner
Pat fish dry with paper towels. Mix salt, ginger and pepper. Coat both sides of fish with this mixture and rub 1 teaspoon cornstarch on both sides of fish. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Place black beans in a bowl and cover with warm water. Stir about 2 minutes. Remove and drain. Discard the water.
Cut the remaining green onion into thin slices (strings).
Remove seeds and membranes from chilies. Cut chilies into very thin slices.
Mix 1 tablespoon cornstarch, water and sugar.
If grilled fish is preferred, grill on charcoal for 4-5 minutes per side or 10 minutes per inch of thickness, not too close to the coals - otherwise fry in a wok.
Heat wok until hot and add 2 tablespoons oil, tilting to coat the sides. Fry fish for 2 minutes or until brown, turning once. Reduce heat to low, cover and simmer for 10 minutes, turning after 3 minutes. Uncover and remove from wok.
Bring wok back up to very hot over high heat. Add 1 tablespoon oil, tilt and coat. Add black beans, chilies, garlic and green onion pieces and stir fry all for 1 minute. Add broth/stock and heat to boiling. Stir in the cornstarch/sugar water mixture, stirring until thickened. Add fish steaks, turning to coat with sauce. Heat for 2 minutes.
Line platter with spinach or lettuce leaves. Place fish on the bed and garnish top with green onion slivers.
